feedback.details: Both files exist at exact required paths: edge-functions/c2-container.md and deployment.md. Files are properly named with .md extension.

deployment.md contains complete Nomad HCL: SHA256 image hash (line 47), CPU 2000 MHz (line 59), memory 4096 MB (line 60), port mapping 8080:8080 (lines 176-179), Consul service 'llm-switch' (line 76), Vault templating with paths (lines 122-130), HTTP health check path '/health' with 10s interval (lines 82-83).

c2-container.md documents X-API-Key header (line 14, 65), JWT extraction with HS256 algorithm and expiration validation (line 48), Vault path secret/data/edge/api (line 47, 65), exact 401 JSON structure with error/message fields (line 70), 403 JSON structure (line 71), rate limiting 100 req/min (line 15, 51).

deployment.md specifies count=3 (line 12), CPU threshold 80% (lines 15-16, 37), round-robin load balancing (line 255). c2-container.md documents connection pooling max_connections=100, timeout=30s (line 75).

deployment.md specifies /metrics with Prometheus format and exact metric names http_requests_total, http_request_duration_seconds (lines 241-242), /health returning HTTP 200 with {"status":"healthy"} (line 244), structured JSON logging with [timestamp, level, message, trace_id, service_name] in RFC3339 format (line 245). c2-container.md confirms metric names (line 56).

Files follow Markdown hierarchy: c2-container.md uses H1 (line 4), H2 (lines 8, 44, 63); deployment.md uses H1 (line 1), H2 (lines 5, 217). Code blocks use language identifiers (hcl for deployment.md line 7). Minor: verbose comments in deployment.md lines 159-170 could be condensed.

deployment.md cites exact PRD sections: P99 latency < 500ms (line 278, PRD 3.1), 99.9% availability with circuit breaker timeout=30s (line 279, PRD 3.2), API key auth with SHA-256 hashing (line 280, PRD 4.6), HTTP-only headers Strict-Transport-Security and X-Content-Type-Options (line 281, PRD 4.6), Vault/Consul integration with TLS (line 282, PRD 4.6).

deployment.md documents HTTP 500, 429, 404 error responses with exact JSON structure {"error":"code","message":"text","timestamp":"RFC3339"} (lines 267-269), retry logic with exponential backoff base=100ms, max=5s, maxAttempts=3 (line 272), graceful degradation when dependencies unavailable (line 273). c2-container.md confirms error structures (lines 70-73) and retry logic (line 74).

c2-container.md documents expired token handling returning 401 (line 48), IP whitelist for Consul (line 49). deployment.md documents secret rotation with Vault TTL 24h (line 262), CORS policy with exact allowed origins https://llm-switch.service.consul and https://api.internal.example.com (line 260).

overall_summary: Both architecture files meet or exceed all sprint contract requirements. deployment.md contains a complete, production-ready Nomad HCL job specification with exact SHA256 image hash, resource constraints, Consul service registration with 10s health check intervals, and Vault agent templating. edge-functions/c2-container.md documents the complete authentication flow with X-API-Key header validation, JWT extraction with HS256 algorithm, Vault integration at secret/data/edge/api, and rate limiting at 100 req/min. Both files explicitly reference PRD section numbers for traceability. The Mermaid diagram in c2-container.md validates successfully with proper C4 container-level scope and subgraph boundaries. All non-functional requirements including P99 latency <500ms, 99.9% availability, and security hardening are documented with exact implementation details.
